sections.heading: Page 1
paragraphs:
GOVSECURE
AI Use Case Intake Checklist
A practical step-by-step checklist for capturing the right information before an AI use case is submitted, triaged, approved, or sent back for
cleanup.
Purpose
Use this checklist to make sure the intake process covers the business objective, AI tool profile, data exposure, decision impact, risks, required controls, and
approval readiness. It is designed for SMB governance teams that want discipline without red tape.
Document Type Operational Checklist Template Version 1.0
Prepared For {{COMPANY_NAME}} Owner {{AI_GOVERNANCE_LEAD}}
At least annually or upon process
Review Cycle Related Artifact AI Use Case Registry
change
Business owners, AI requestors, IT,
Primary Users Status Draft / Approved / In Use
security, privacy, legal
How to use this checklist
 Complete the checklist during the intake process - not after the use case is already live.
 If an item cannot be completed, document the gap and identify who must close it before review.
 Use the Notes / Evidence column for links, supporting documents, screenshots, or contract references.
 Any answered risk trigger should be routed into the AI risk triage workflow and the AI Use Case Registry.
GovSecure | Checklist Library | AI Use Case Intake

4. Understand the data profile
Most intake failures happen here. If the team cannot explain the data, the review should not pretend otherwise.
Check Required step or confirmation What should be covered Notes / evidence

Common failure point
If the requestor cannot explain what data goes in, what outputs come out, and whether the vendor trains on that data, the intake is not complete. That is not a minor detail.
That is the review.

6. Run the initial risk screen
This is the fast filter that tells the governance team whether the request is simple, sensitive, or likely to need escalation.
Check Required step or confirmation What should be covered Notes / evidence
Check for sensitive or regulated data If yes, route the request for deeper
[ ]

[ ]

Escalation note
Any yes-answer tied to sensitive data, customer or employee impact, automated decisions, or regulated use should trigger deeper review. The checklist is a front door, not a
loophole.
